column2: # Step 1: Separate the data into two groups (Production and Comprehension)
mutate(eng_level = factor(eng_level, levels = c("EC", "AL", "PL")))  # Ensure order is correct
# Step 2: Set consistent y-axis limits and ticks
y_breaks <- seq(0, 0.5, by = 0.05)  # Set y-axis ticks at 0.05 intervals
# Step 3: Create the boxplot for Production and Comprehension
plot_prod_comp <- ggplot(prod_comp_data, aes(x = eng_level, y = percentage, fill = condition)) +  # Use fill instead of color
geom_boxplot() +  # Boxplots with no outlier shapes
facet_grid(. ~ event_type, scales = "free", space = "free") +  # Facet by event type
scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits, breaks = y_breaks, sec.axis = dup_axis(name = NULL)) +  # Consistent y-axis limits and ticks
scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +  # Use fill instead of color for the Brewer palette
labs(x = "", y = "Average Percentage", fill = "Condition") +  # Change legend label from color to fill
panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"))+  # Adjust space between panels
